library(limma)
library(protools2)

library(ggplot2)
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2

For each dataset: 1) Perform differential phosphosite analysis by Limma, e.g., in Dataset1, two groups were listed in the column names, i.e., “GSK.Trametinib” and “GSK.DMSO” groups; In Dataset2, two groups were listed in the column names, “DMSO.Trametinib” and “DMSO.DMSO”.

df1 <-read.csv(
  "Data/Dataset 1 for assingment phospho of LSD1i pretreated cells followwed by trametinib treatment.csv")

df2 <-read.csv(
  "Data/Dataset 2 for assingment phospho of control pretreated cells followwed by trametinib treatment.csv")

head(df1)
##                 sites P31Fuj_GSK.DMSO_PP1_R1 P31Fuj_GSK.DMSO_PP1_R2

head(df2)
##                 sites P31Fuj_DMSO.DMSO_PP1_R1 P31Fuj_DMSO.DMSO_PP1_R2

## 6       PTPN14(Y589);                2.92e+01                32.00000

## 4                1.64e+01                       23.0000

## 6                3.60e+01                       46.5000
##   P31Fuj_DMSO.Trametinib_PP1_R2 P31Fuj_DMSO.Trametinib_PP2_R1
## 1                       0.00342                       0.00687
## 2                     102.00000                     129.00000
## 3                     134.50000                     118.80000
## 4                      21.80000                      14.20000

## 6                      37.70000                      30.60000
##   P31Fuj_DMSO.Trametinib_PP2_R2 P31Fuj_DMSO.Trametinib_PP3_R1
## 1                          1.89                       0.00601
## 2                        137.00                     101.50000
## 3                        121.50                     132.90000
## 4                         12.10                      22.40000

## 6                         31.30                      42.10000
##   P31Fuj_DMSO.Trametinib_PP3_R2
## 1                      0.948435
## 2                    133.000000
## 3                    120.150000
## 4                     13.150000
## 5                      2.190000
## 6                     30.950000
df1.norm <- data.frame(
  site = df1$sites,
  scale(log2(df1[, 2:ncol(df1)]), scale = FALSE)
)

df2.norm <- data.frame(
  site = df2$sites,
  scale(log2(df2[, 2:ncol(df2)]), scale = FALSE)
)

Statistical Analysis

#DATA1
control_samples_1 <- colnames(df1.norm)[grep("GSK.DMSO", colnames(df1.norm))]
test_samples_1    <- colnames(df1.norm)[grep("GSK.Trametinib", colnames(df1.norm))]

df1.limma <- protools2::compare.by.limma(
  df.to.compare   = df1.norm,
  control.samples = control_samples_1,
  test.samples    = test_samples_1
)

#Question didnt ask for it , but i run it in case we need it in next questions.
write.csv(df1.limma, "Results of limma analysis of data1.csv", row.names = F)
head(df1.limma[order(df1.limma$difference.test.vs.control), ])
##                       protein difference.test.vs.control      pvalues
## 1982              ETV3(S132);                  -12.15726 3.460967e-13
## 7001 MAPK3(T207);MAPK3(Y204);                  -11.02676 3.019411e-11
## 7002             MAPK3(Y204);                  -11.02676 3.019411e-11
## 7003             MAPK3(T202);                  -10.97357 2.130601e-13
## 806              MAPK1(T185);                  -10.86033 5.409026e-07
## 6136          RAB3GAP1(T542);                  -10.70417 3.604398e-06
##               FDR
## 1982 1.811816e-09
## 7001 6.322646e-08
## 7002 6.322646e-08
## 7003 1.811816e-09
## 806  3.552266e-04
## 6136 1.451463e-03
df1.significant <- subset(df1.limma, df1.limma$FDR < 0.05)

df1.significant.decreased <- subset(
  df1.significant,
  df1.significant$difference.test.vs.control < 0
)

df1.significant.increased <- subset(
  df1.significant,
  df1.significant$difference.test.vs.control > 0
)

#Question didnt ask for it , but i run it in case we need it in next questions.
nrow(df1.significant.decreased)
## [1] 78
nrow(df1.significant.increased)
## [1] 4
#DATA2
control_samples_2 <- colnames(df2.norm)[grep("DMSO.DMSO", colnames(df2.norm))]
test_samples_2    <- colnames(df2.norm)[grep("DMSO.Trametinib", colnames(df2.norm))]

df2.limma <- protools2::compare.by.limma(
  df.to.compare   = df2.norm,
  control.samples = control_samples_2,
  test.samples    = test_samples_2
)


#Question didnt ask for it , but i run it in case we need it in next questions.
write.csv(df2.limma, "Results of limma analysis of data2.csv", row.names = F)
head(df2.limma[order(df2.limma$difference.test.vs.control), ])
##                       protein difference.test.vs.control      pvalues
## 1982              ETV3(S132);                  -12.44154 4.935947e-15
## 806              MAPK1(T185);                  -12.01280 1.043396e-12
## 805              MAPK1(Y187);                  -11.62871 2.094374e-12
## 7003             MAPK3(T202);                  -11.39661 5.038051e-13
## 7001 MAPK3(T207);MAPK3(Y204);                  -11.20582 1.969466e-12
## 7002             MAPK3(Y204);                  -11.20582 1.969466e-12
##               FDR
## 1982 5.167937e-11
## 806  9.931237e-10
## 805  1.566292e-09
## 7003 5.274839e-10
## 7001 1.566292e-09
## 7002 1.566292e-09
df2.significant <- subset(df2.limma, df2.limma$FDR < 0.05)

df2.significant.decreased <- subset(
  df2.significant,
  df2.significant$difference.test.vs.control < 0
)

df2.significant.increased <- subset(
  df2.significant,
  df2.significant$difference.test.vs.control > 0
)


#Question didnt ask for it , but i run it in case we need it in next questions.
nrow(df2.significant.decreased)
## [1] 288
nrow(df2.significant.increased)
## [1] 48
  1. Use KSEA to determine the kinase(s) differentially activated or deactivated by trametinib
#DATA1
df1.k <- protools2::kinase.substrate.enrichment(
  dfx = df1.limma,
  ks_db = "pdts"
)

#View top kinases in df1.k
df1.k[order(df1.k$pvalue), ]

  dfx = df2.limma,
  ks_db = "pdts")

## 43     LATS1_pdts 0.02637095
  1. Visualise the results using appropriate tables and plots
#To make sure about kinases column
colnames(df1.k)
## [1] "kinases"        "zscores"        "pvalues"        "m"             
## [5] "distance"       "sites"          "kinase_dataset" "qvalue"
#DATA1
k1.selected <- subset(df1.k, df1.k$pvalues < .01)
plot.ksea1 <- ggplot(df1.k, aes(x = zscores, y = -log10(qvalue))) +
  geom_point(aes(size = m)) +
  geom_text(
    data = k1.selected,
    aes(x = zscores, y = -log10(qvalue), label = kinases),
    hjust = -0.2
  ) +
  labs(title = "KSEA of trametinib-treated cells (LSD1i pretreated)")

plot.ksea1

#DATA2
k2.selected <- subset(df2.k, df2.k$pvalues < .01)
plot.ksea2 <- ggplot(df2.k, aes(x = zscores, y = -log10(qvalue))) +
  geom_point(aes(size = m)) +
  geom_text(
    data = k2.selected,
    aes(x = zscores, y = -log10(qvalue), label = kinases),
    hjust = -0.2
  ) +
  labs(title = "KSEA of trametinib-treated cells (control pretreated)")

plot.ksea2

  1. Name the top 10 phosphorylation sites decreased and increased by the kinase inhibitor in each dataset
#DATA1

top10_df1_decreased <- head(
  df1.significant[
    order(df1.significant$difference.test.vs.control),
  ],
  10
)

top10_df1_decreased
##                           protein difference.test.vs.control      pvalues
## 1982                  ETV3(S132);                 -12.157258 3.460967e-13
## 7001     MAPK3(T207);MAPK3(Y204);                 -11.026755 3.019411e-11
## 7002                 MAPK3(Y204);                 -11.026755 3.019411e-11
## 7003                 MAPK3(T202);                 -10.973572 2.130601e-13
## 806                  MAPK1(T185);                 -10.860331 5.409026e-07
## 6136              RAB3GAP1(T542);                 -10.704170 3.604398e-06
## 805                  MAPK1(Y187);                 -10.592807 4.235827e-07
## 1259                 DUSP6(S351);                  -9.899835 1.248926e-12
## 7750                   ERF(T526);                  -9.830993 6.484653e-07
## 109  NCAPD2(S1330);NCAPD2(T1331);                  -8.590714 4.190784e-06
##               FDR
## 1982 1.811816e-09
## 7001 6.322646e-08
## 7002 6.322646e-08
## 7003 1.811816e-09
## 806  3.552266e-04
## 6136 1.451463e-03
## 805  3.167794e-04
## 1259 4.358751e-09
## 7750 3.993783e-04
## 109  1.625093e-03
top10_df1_increased <- head(
  df1.significant[
    order(df1.significant$difference.test.vs.control, decreasing = TRUE),
  ],
  10
)

top10_df1_increased
##             protein difference.test.vs.control      pvalues          FDR
## 1230   OSTF1(S213);                  2.7749149 5.092272e-08 5.331609e-05
## 1114    LSP1(T184);                  1.5709117 1.246667e-06 7.128224e-04
## 6224    NASP(T700);                  1.5051786 8.909735e-05 1.696090e-02
## 9217  SLC12A2(T75);                  0.6611257 3.439131e-04 4.487252e-02
## 1371   KIF4A(T803);                 -0.5535752 1.199418e-04 1.931986e-02
## 1631    PAK1(T214);                 -0.6224348 1.035986e-04 1.870134e-02
## 1584   ATG2B(S495);                 -0.6950333 1.319506e-04 2.061974e-02
## 1306   NDRG3(S341);                 -0.7045821 2.862978e-04 3.944128e-02
## 489     ABL2(T938);                 -0.7233531 1.102881e-04 1.924527e-02
## 6215 RAP1GAP2(T38);                 -0.7507219 7.564187e-05 1.583941e-02
cat("Top 10 decreased (Dataset 1):",
    paste(top10_df1_decreased$protein, collapse = ", "))
## Top 10 decreased (Dataset 1): ETV3(S132);, MAPK3(T207);MAPK3(Y204);, MAPK3(Y204);, MAPK3(T202);, MAPK1(T185);, RAB3GAP1(T542);, MAPK1(Y187);, DUSP6(S351);, ERF(T526);, NCAPD2(S1330);NCAPD2(T1331);
cat("\n----------\n")
## 
## ----------
cat("Top 10 increased (Dataset 1):",
    paste(top10_df1_increased$protein, collapse = ", "))
## Top 10 increased (Dataset 1): OSTF1(S213);, LSP1(T184);, NASP(T700);, SLC12A2(T75);, KIF4A(T803);, PAK1(T214);, ATG2B(S495);, NDRG3(S341);, ABL2(T938);, RAP1GAP2(T38);

  1. Name the top kinases increased by treatment in each dataset
# I already have df1.k df2.k and k1.selected k2.selected (pvalue < 0.01)


top_kinases_df1 <- subset(k1.selected, zscores > 0)
top_kinases_df1 <- top_kinases_df1[order(top_kinases_df1$zscores, decreasing = TRUE), ]

cat("Top kinases increased by trametinib (Dataset 1):",
    paste(top_kinases_df1$kinases, collapse = ", "))
## Top kinases increased by trametinib (Dataset 1): IRAK1, CIT, TAOK3, CAMKK2, ERN1, ARAF
cat("\n-----------\n")
## 
## -----------
top_kinases_df2 <- subset(k2.selected, zscores > 0)
top_kinases_df2 <- top_kinases_df2[order(top_kinases_df2$zscores, decreasing = TRUE), ]

cat("Top kinases increased by trametinib (Dataset 2):",
    paste(top_kinases_df2$kinases, collapse = ", "))
## Top kinases increased by trametinib (Dataset 2): CDK9
  1. Name the top kinases decreased by treatment in each dataset
decreased_kinases_df1 <- subset(k1.selected, zscores < 0)
decreased_kinases_df1 <- decreased_kinases_df1[order(decreased_kinases_df1$zscores), ]

cat("Kinases decreased by trametinib (Dataset 1):",
    paste(decreased_kinases_df1$kinases, collapse = ", "))
## Kinases decreased by trametinib (Dataset 1): MAP2K1, MAPK1, MAPK3, TNK2
cat("\n-----------\n")
## 
## -----------
decreased_kinases_df2 <- subset(k2.selected, zscores < 0)
decreased_kinases_df2 <- decreased_kinases_df2[order(decreased_kinases_df2$zscores), ]

cat("Kinases decreased by trametinib (Dataset 2):",
    paste(decreased_kinases_df2$kinases, collapse = ", "))
## Kinases decreased by trametinib (Dataset 2): PRKCI, LATS1, ARAF
  1. What impact did the pre-treatment with the LSD1 inhibitor had in the effect of trametinib in decreasing kinase activities?
decreased_kinases_df1$kinases
## [1] "MAP2K1" "MAPK1"  "MAPK3"  "TNK2"
decreased_kinases_df2$kinases
## [1] "PRKCI" "LATS1" "ARAF"
decreased_kinases_df1[, c("kinases", "zscores")]
##    kinases    zscores
## 30  MAP2K1 -1.1974969
## 21   MAPK1 -0.6725737
## 22   MAPK3 -0.6725737
## 10    TNK2 -0.2440318
decreased_kinases_df2[, c("kinases", "zscores")]
##    kinases     zscores
## 5    PRKCI -1.18493065
## 43   LATS1 -0.91968810
## 1     ARAF -0.03847141
#Pre-treatment with an LSD1 inhibitor made the cells more sensitive to trametinib.

#This action brought on a strong and coordinated inhibition of the MAPK pathway. This included the reduced activity for MEK1 and also ERK1/2.

#If there was no LSD1 inhibition, the trametinib was unable to suppress the MAPK signalling effectively. Instead, it mainly affected the kinases that are not part of the MAPK system.
